title: Debug LiveView: handle_info from a supervised task loses sandbox
prompt: Write `docs/lv_task_sandbox_loss.md` diagnosing this scenario: a LiveView `handle_event` spawns a `Task.Supervisor.async_nolink` child that queries the Repo. In production it works; in tests the child's Repo.all raises `DBConnection.OwnershipError`. Must explain: (1) the LV process has the sandbox allowance from the `on_mount(:sandbox)` hook, (2) `Task.Supervisor.async_nolink` spawns under a DIFFERENT supervisor — the child does NOT inherit the LV's sandbox allowance, (3) the fix is to either use `Task.async_nolink/1` (stays under LV process $callers) OR call `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow/3` manually on the child pid in the test.
tier_rationale: Requires synthesizing LiveView process model + $callers semantics + sandbox allowance propagation.
research_citation: "The sandbox uses the mechanic described here to track the caller (aka the test process) for a LV" — Elixir Forum, LostKobrakai
